title: ## TASK Code quality review of the telop-pack-srt-02 video pipeline core modules. ## EXPECTED OUTCOME 7-point quality score with specific issues and suggestions. ## CONTEXT ### Key Files: 1. src/brand.ts (262 lines): Centralized brand tokens - colors, typography, motion, spacing - 6 style configs, p...
summary_text: { "scores": { "accuracy": 1, "performance": 2, "maintainability": 1 }, "total": 4, "issues": [ { "severity": "critical", "file": "src/services/bridge/template-router.ts", "line": "16", "description": "Implementation does not match the stated/expected router behavior (no bidirectional mapping, no primary/secondary preferences, no round-robin rotation; only keyword/accent scoring across 6 styles). This makes selection behavior likely incorrect vs spec and hard to reason about.", "suggestion": "Align code/comments/spec: either implement the missing behaviors (tie-handling + round-robin among top scores; explicit scene-type preferences) or rewrite the header/docs and mapping tables so they describe the current algorithm accurately." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "src/services/bridge/template-router.ts", "line": "17", "description": "KEYWORD_MAP comments and style assignments appear semantically mismatched (e.g., \"Y2K Glitch\" -> \"03_Cinematic\", \"Paper Collage\" -> \"04_CyberGlitch\"), which is likely to route scripts to the wrong style.", "suggestion": "Audit keyword/style pairs and ACCENT_MAP against actual template visuals and rename/update comments so each mapping reflects the real TelopStyle." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "src/services/bridge/template-router.ts", "line": "44", "description": "Immutability rule violation: scores are mutated via \"+=\" across multiple functions, making it easy to introduce hidden state bugs and violating the stated coding standard.", "suggestion": "Refactor scoring to return new score objects (e.g., have scoreFromMetadata/scoreFromKeywords return a new scores record) or at minimum confine mutation to a single local function and return a fresh cloned object at the end." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "src/services/planner/orchestrator.ts", "line": "75", "description": "Error handling loses all context: catch blocks swallow errors and silently fall back, making production failures indistinguishable from “LLM returned bad JSON”.", "suggestion": "Capture the error in `catch (err)` and propagate a non-logging signal (e.g., return `{...fallback, _meta:{fallbackReason:\"parse_failed\"}}` if your types allow, or rethrow a typed error with `cause` in non-fallback callers)." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "src/services/planner/orchestrator.ts", "line": "258", "description": "JSON extraction is too weak for real LLM outputs: extractJson only strips code fences, but many models emit leading/trailing text; this will frequently trigger fallback even when valid JSON exists in the response.", "suggestion": "Implement a safer extractor (e.g., locate first '{' and parse with brace balancing; or attempt `JSON.parse` on progressively trimmed substrings) before falling back." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "src/services/image/orchestrator.ts", "line": "30", "description": "Immutability rule violation: `imagePaths` is mutated via `.push()` inside a loop.", "suggestion": "Build results immutably (e.g., map scenes to promises, filter nulls, then return the resulting array) while keeping write order deterministic." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "src/services/image/orchestrator.ts", "line": "25", "description": "`generateImages()` only generates SVGs when `scene.image` is present, yet `generateSceneSvg()` itself already has a default (`scene.image ?? \"abstract background\"`). This mismatch is easy to misunderstand and can lead to missing images even when a background-only SVG would be acceptable.", "suggestion": "Either generate an SVG for every scene (using the defaults) or remove the nullish defaults and make “only when image prompt exists” an explicit contract in naming/docs." }, { "severity": "major", "file": "src/brand.ts", "line": "190", "description": "BRAND_BY_STYLE and BASE_* objects are shared references; callers can mutate returned tokens, potentially corrupting global style state (and violating immutability expectations).", "suggestion": "Return read-only/frozen tokens: make exports...
